pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot parent version to 3.3.13 to align with Java 21 and Spring Boot 3.3 requirements.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.0.x does not support Java 21 as a baseline; upgrading is required for compatibility with the target JDK.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update Java version property to 21 to compile and run on JDK 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to target Java 17, failing to utilize or comply with Java 21 features and potentially causing runtime issues if Java 21-specific APIs are used.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Cloud version to 2023.0.6 to match Spring Boot 3.3.x compatibility.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Cloud 2022.0.0 is incompatible with Spring Boot 3.3.x, leading to dependency resolution conflicts or runtime errors.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace Apache HttpClient 4.x with HttpClient 5.x to align with modern Spring Boot dependencies.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.x and newer versions of Spring Cloud often deprecate or remove support for HttpClient 4.x, requiring migration to HttpClient 5.x for compatibility.

src/main/java/app/config/WebSecurityConf.general_idea: Update Spring Security configuration to use the new `withDefaults()` API for HTTP Basic authentication.
src/main/java/app/config/WebSecurityConf.why_exists: The method `HttpSecurity.httpBasic()` without arguments is deprecated in Spring Security 6 (Spring Boot 3.x) and may be removed in future versions; using `withDefaults()` is the required modern pattern.

src/main/java/com/mpalourdio/springboott.general_idea: Replace deprecated List.get(0) with List.getFirst() for cleaner collection access.

src/test/java/com/mpalourdio/springboott.general_idea: Replace legacy List.get(0) call with Java 21's List.getFirst() method for improved readability.
Replace deprecated List.get(0) with List.getFirst() for modern Java API usage.
Replace deprecated List.get(0) with List.getFirst() for modern Java 21 API usage.
Replace List.get(0) with List.getFirst() to utilize the new Java 21 API.
